chapter_context.summary: Discusses business honesty, the guidance of integrity, the danger of talebearers, the value of wise counsel, and the liberal soul being made fat.

outline.text: Just Weights, Pride, and the Guidance of Integrity
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Just Weights, Pride, and the Guidance of Integrity (11:1-8), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

outline.text: The Talebearer, Counseling, and Surety Warnings
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Talebearer, Counseling, and Surety Warnings (11:9-15), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

outline.text: A Gracious Woman, Cruel Men, and the Reward of Righteousness
outline.explanation: This section focuses on A Gracious Woman, Cruel Men, and the Reward of Righteousness (11:16-23), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

outline.text: Liberal Giving, Trusting in Riches, and Fruit of the Righteous
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Liberal Giving, Trusting in Riches, and Fruit of the Righteous (11:24-31), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

key_themes.title: Wisdom vs. Folly
key_themes.description: Proverbs outlines a practical blueprint for daily living, contrasting the actions, words, and consequences of the wise with those of the foolish.

key_themes.title: The Fear of the Lord
key_themes.description: The foundational principle of wisdom is a reverent awe and trust in God, which guides moral choices and shapes character.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the practical sayings of Chapter 11, what is one specific area of your speech, relationships, or work where you can apply divine wisdom today?
How does this chapter prompt you to choose the path of integrity and diligence over convenience or compromise?

cultural_historical.title: Ancient Near Eastern Wisdom Literature
cultural_historical.text: Proverbs shares structural and thematic similarities with other ancient wisdom writings, but stands unique in placing the Fear of Yahweh as the foundation of all true wisdom.
